---
title: "Lesson Splitting - {{Lesson Name}}"
id: lesson_splitting_{{lesson_name}}
author: brad_cannell
date: {{YYYY-MM-DD}}
date-modified: {{YYYY-MM-DD}}
audience: course_authors
category: lesson_development
tags:
  - r
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  eval: true # Switch to false to run interactively
---

# Overview

- Conversation about splitting a lesson into multiple lessons.

# Prerequisites

See [Working with Python and Chatlas](https://brad-cannell.github.io/my-ai-assistant/vignettes/python-and-chatlas.html) for setup instructions (virtual environment, package installation, API key management).

# Load Packages

```{python}
from chatlas import ChatAnthropic, ChatOpenAI
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
import keyring
import pickle
import os
```

# File Paths and Environment Variables

```{python}
# OpenAI Models: https://platform.openai.com/docs/models
# Anthropic Models: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview
# 2026-02-27: We prefer claude for lesson revisions.
model = "claude-opus-4-6"

# Today's date
today = date.today().isoformat()

# Course and lesson name
# By default, these are injected from Convert Chapters.qmd
# They can also be altered manually.
course_name = "{{Course Name}}"
chapter_name = "" # Fill manually
lesson_name = "{{Lesson Name}}"

# Lesson file and folder paths
# By default, these are injected from Convert Chapters.qmd
# They can also be altered manually.
lesson_file_path = Path("{{lesson_file_path}}")
lesson_folder_path = lesson_file_path.parent

# Lesson content
lesson_content = (lesson_file_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
```

```{python}
# Check that all paths exist
paths_to_check = {
    "lesson_file_path": lesson_file_path,
    "lesson_folder_path": lesson_folder_path
}

for name, path in paths_to_check.items():
    if not path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Path not found — {name}: {path}")
    print(f"✓  {name}: {path}")
```

# Load helper functions

See [Python Helper Functions](https://brad-cannell.github.io/my-ai-assistant/vignettes/python-helper-functions.html) for installation instructions and a full function reference.

```{python}
from py_scripts import (
    prompt_extract_system_prompt,
    prompt_extract_user_message,
    prompt_fill_placeholders,
)
```

# Expose API

See [Storing API Keys with Keyring](https://brad-cannell.github.io/my-ai-assistant/vignettes/keyring.html) for first-time setup instructions.

```{python}
# Retrieve the password from the system keyring - Claude
claude_api = keyring.get_password("ewb-course-development-claude", "brad")

if claude_api is not None:
    # Set the retrieved value as a temporary environment variable
    os.environ['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = claude_api
    print("Environment variable 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' set successfully within the script.")
else:
    print("Could not retrieve password from keyring.")
```

# Create Chat Object

The `system_prompt` argument is optional. Omit it for a one-off conversation, or pass a string to set a persistent role or constraints. See [System Prompts](https://brad-cannell.github.io/my-ai-assistant/vignettes/system-prompts.html) for details.

```{python}
chat = ChatAnthropic(
    model = model,
    system_prompt = "You are an expert instructional editor specializing in online and asynchronous learning. Your role is to revise course language to improve clarity, precision, tone, and readability while preserving the original instructional intent, learning objectives, and technical accuracy."
)
```

# Pass a Prompt to the AI Assistant

```{python}
user_message = f"""
I'm working on a new Epi-Workbench course called {course_name}. The course has a chapter named {chapter_name}. Currently, there is just one lesson file for this chapter. However, I want to divide it into two or more lessons. Please suggest how I should break up the content and what I should name the lessons.

Here is the lesson content:

{lesson_content}
"""
chat.chat(user_message, echo='none')
```

## Response

```{python}
#| echo: false
print(chat.get_last_turn())
```

# Follow-up and Refinement

A section for optional follow-up questions and refinements. 
- Optionally, create a fork of the chat. `import copy; chat_fork = copy.deepcopy(chat)`
- Asks follow-up questions in interactive mode - `chat.app()`.

# Save Chat History

See [Saving and Resuming Chats](https://brad-cannell.github.io/my-ai-assistant/vignettes/save-and-resume-chats.html) for the full workflow. Save both formats: markdown for browsing, pickle for resuming.

```{python}
# Markdown — for browsing and searching
chat_history_path_md = conversation_folder_path / ("chat_history_" + today + ".md")
chat.export(chat_history_path_md, overwrite=True)

# Pickle — for resuming in a later session
chat_history_path_pkl = conversation_folder_path / ("chat_history_" + today + ".pkl")
with open(chat_history_path_pkl, "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(chat.get_turns(), f)
```
